sanskrit: यो न हृष्यति न द्वेष्टि न शोचति न काङ्क्षति। शुभाशुभपरित्यागी भक्तिमान्यः स मे प्रियः॥
transliteration: yo na hṛṣyati na dveṣṭi na śocati na kāṅkṣati | śubhāśubha-parityāgī bhaktimānyaḥ sa me priyaḥ ||
literal_meaning: Who neither exults nor hates, neither grieves nor desires, renouncing good and evil—endowed with devotion, he is dear to Me.
contextual_meaning: Krishna describes the psychologically de-conditioned devotee whose equanimity is no longer hijacked by the hedonic or moral valence of events; affect is stable because identity is anchored beyond the egoic appraisal system.
psychological_state: Post-ambivalent emotional regulation; observer mode activated; egoic appraisal network offline; secure attachment to transcendental object (Krishna).
trigger_scenario: You are ghosted by a close friend, win a random prize, and get criticized at work—all in one day—and your mood stays flat, curious, and softly compassionate rather than elated or crushed.
anchor_text: Stay even-minded through joy and pain, free from judging good or bad.
